mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives immediate positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising industrial production confirms a synchronized demand-supply expansion, validating corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening, rising short-term rates compress equity valuations and increase borrowing costs, which mechanically drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn positive in more than 65% of triggered tightening episodes, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels and term spread.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts count exceeding 1.3 million units signals robust consumer confidence and construction activity, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 50% of cases where HOUST breaches 1300, or the monthly Rank IC drops below 0.02 across three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic momentum, which directly supports equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) when PAYEMS shows consecutive monthly gains, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales coupled with a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based consumer strength, which historically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the cross-product term in a multivariate regression loses statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, sustained industrial production growth indicates resilient corporate capacity utilization, which supports earnings stability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month despite valuation concerns.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of late-cycle episodes where INDPRO remains above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for term spread.
